summary: Nimbus lacks explicit validation that Fulu/Gloas forks implement exactly the EIPs mandated by EIP-7607. Invariant relies on manual correctness and external test coverage. No exploitable bug exists in current code, but architectural gap increases hardfork implementation risk.

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Abstract analysis of fork configuration state space reveals no explicit EIP feature enumeration or validation mechanism in Nimbus client.

state_anomalies_found.description: RuntimeConfig defines fork epochs (FULU_FORK_EPOCH, GLOAS_FORK_EPOCH) but does not enumerate or validate which EIPs are included
state_anomalies_found.abstract_state: Fork activation controlled by epoch thresholds only, no EIP feature flags

state_anomalies_found.description: Code assumes all clients implementing Fulu/Gloas forks have identical feature sets based on hard fork name alone
state_anomalies_found.abstract_state: ConsensusFork enum values (Fulu, Gloas) serve as implicit feature set identifiers without explicit EIP mapping

state_anomalies_found.description: No runtime check verifies that datatypes/fulu.nim and datatypes/gloas.nim implement exactly the 15 EIPs mandated by EIP-7607
state_anomalies_found.abstract_state: Feature implementation scattered across multiple modules without centralized manifest

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Symbolic execution identifies a counterexample: Nimbus could diverge from other clients if implementation omits or adds EIPs beyond EIP-7607 specification.

counterexample.path_conditions:
1. Nimbus reaches FULU_FORK_EPOCH
2. Nimbus applies upgrade_to_fulu(cfg, state.electraData.data, cache)
3. upgrade_to_fulu implements only 10 of 11 core EIPs (hypothetical omission)
4. Other clients (Geth, Prysm, Lighthouse) implement all 11 core EIPs
counterexample.expected_outcome: Consensus failure: Nimbus produces different state root after fork transition than other clients. Network partition occurs with Nimbus nodes on minority fork.

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: Divergence would manifest at fork activation epoch, affecting entire network. Not attacker-triggered but results from implementation error.
phase2_5_reachability_analysis.data_flow_path: Block processing -> maybeUpgradeState() -> epoch check -> upgrade_to_fulu() -> apply consensus rules

validation_layers.effectiveness: Validates epoch ordering only, not EIP feature completeness
Validates blob schedule ordering, not EIP inclusion

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: Bug would be triggered by block processing at fork epoch, but root cause is implementation omission/addition, not attacker input. All nodes would diverge simultaneously at predetermined epoch if bug exists.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Cannot prove invariant holds. No formal mechanism enforces EIP-7607 compliance. Invariant relies on informal development process.
phase3_invariant_proving.proof_attempt:
To prove: ∀ clients C1, C2 implementing Fulu fork: EIPs(C1) = EIPs(C2) = {EIP-7594, EIP-7642, ..., EIP-7951}

Required preconditions:
1. Centralized EIP manifest: ABSENT (no single source of truth)
2. Implementation completeness check: ABSENT (no static assertion verifying all EIPs present)
3. Cross-client test vectors: ASSUMED (consensus-spec-tests, but no in-code reference)

Proof FAILS because:
- ConsensusFork.Fulu is a label, not a verified feature set
- No compile-time or runtime assertion checks EIP implementation completeness
- Type system cannot enforce "all 15 EIPs must be implemented"
- Manual code inspection required to verify compliance

Invariant holds ONLY IF:
- All EIP implementations exist in datatypes/fulu.nim, datatypes/gloas.nim, and related modules
- Consensus-spec-tests catch any omissions (external dependency)
- No client-specific optimizations skip EIP features

phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: Not eligible for bug bounty (requires implementation error, not attacker exploit). However, this is a CRITICAL architectural concern for Ethereum Foundation client development teams.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: This finding represents a PROCESS GAP rather than an exploitable vulnerability. No attacker can trigger divergence; divergence would only occur if Nimbus team implemented Fulu/Gloas incorrectly. However, the lack of automated validation increases risk of human error during hardfork implementation.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.actionable_recommendation:
Add compile-time assertion validating EIP feature completeness:

# In beacon_chain/spec/datatypes/fulu.nim:
const FUSAKA_CORE_EIPS = [
  "EIP-7594", "EIP-7642", "EIP-7823", "EIP-7825",
  "EIP-7883", "EIP-7892", "EIP-7910", "EIP-7917",
  "EIP-7918", "EIP-7934", "EIP-7935"
]
static:
  # Compile-time check that all features are present
  doAssert IMPLEMENTED_EIPS == FUSAKA_CORE_EIPS
